title: dianthus rupicola subsp. aeolicus
intro: Dianthus rupicola subsp. aeolicus, also known as the Aeolian pink, is a subspecies of the Dianthus rupicola plant, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Dianthus rupicola subsp. aeolicus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
